title: Why This Matters in HCI
thesis: HCI reorganization often appears first as boundary restructuring before stable topic labels become dominant.
mainParagraph: Because HCI combines design, engineering, social inquiry, and application domains, change often emerges as cross-community adjacency before lexical consolidation.
secondaryParagraph: Persistent loops provide an explicit way to describe these intermediate structural states without implying causal certainty or inevitable field formation.
cautionNote: This is structural interpretation, not deterministic prediction of HCI's future.
figureCaption: Three-panel schematic: stable topic, emerging adjacency, unresolved boundary loop.

title: Topology: Minimal Background
thesis: Topology here is a pedagogical grammar for reading boundary connectedness, persistence, and closure year by year.
mainParagraph: Birth, persistence, and death indicate when unresolved loop conditions appear, remain, and close under filtration thresholds.
secondaryParagraph: Yearly summaries describe annual structural condition, while representative cycles remain explanatory witnesses rather than identity tracking of one loop over time.
cautionNote: Yearly summaries are computed per year and do not track identical loop objects across time.
figureCaption: Mini loop construction and closure schematic for beginner reading.

title: Positioning Within Existing Approaches
thesis: Topology-based reading extends established bibliometric and semantic methods with boundary-focused interpretation.
mainParagraph: Each approach contributes distinct strengths, but unresolved boundary structures remain difficult to read when only volume, citation, or nearest-neighbor signals are used.
secondaryParagraph: This project adds a structural lens that emphasizes interpretable boundary conditions.
figureCaption: Comparison matrix showing methodological continuity across approach families.

title: Data and Operationalization
thesis: Interpretation depends on operational definitions that tie every major term to an explicit measurable object and explicit non-claims.
mainParagraph: Concepts, communities, links, cycles, and yearly summaries are each defined in project-specific measurable terms to support reliable discussion.
secondaryParagraph: These definitions constrain claims to corpus structure and prevent drift into causal, institutional, or motivational over-interpretation.
cautionNote: Cross-area is semantic and operational, not institutional interdisciplinarity.

title: Detecting Boundary Structures
thesis: A five-step pipeline moves from yearly concept space to local, meso, and macro outputs.
mainParagraph: The workflow constructs filtration, extracts persistent H1, reconstructs representative cycles, and aggregates yearly summaries for interpretation.
secondaryParagraph: Output scales are explicit: local edges, meso cycles, macro yearly descriptors.
cautionNote: Pipeline outputs are evidence for interpretation, not causal mechanisms.
figureCaption: Stepwise method reveal from concept space to yearly summary.

title: Evidence
thesis: Evidence is strongest when micro interpretability, meso ranking performance, and macro yearly summaries are read together.
mainParagraph: Micro cases show what the structure looks like, meso metrics show comparative ordering utility, and macro summaries contextualize annual structural condition.
secondaryParagraph: This triangulation supports rigorous discussion while keeping causal, identity-tracking, and deterministic claims outside evidence boundaries.
cautionNote: Do not interpret yearly summaries as persistent identity tracking of specific loops.
figureCaption: VR–Therapy cycle, metric cards, and yearly summary framing.

title: How to Read the Outputs
thesis: Birth, persistence, and death offer an interpretive grammar for structural evidence.
mainParagraph: Birth indicates emergence, persistence indicates unresolved boundary condition, and death indicates closure or divergence.
secondaryParagraph: Representative cycles remain explanatory reconstructions rather than unique truths.
cautionNote: Not every disappearing loop becomes a stable field.
figureCaption: State transition diagram: birth → persistence → closure/divergence.

title: Scope and Interpretive Boundaries
thesis: Trust requires explicit claim boundaries: what outputs capture directly, what interpretation can cautiously add, and what remains non-claim.
mainParagraph: The method supports structural claims about adjacency, separation, unresolved loops, and annual summaries when linked to clear measurable objects.
secondaryParagraph: It does not establish causality, intent, institutional organization, unique trajectories, or persistent cross-year loop identity.
cautionNote: Scope clarity is part of the contribution, not a limitation to hide.
figureCaption: Continuum view of support strength from direct evidence to non-claims.

title: Implications for HCI and SciSci
thesis: Topology-based structural reading supports discussion-oriented decisions when combined with explicit scope limits and multi-scale evidence.
mainParagraph: The method contributes a calm analytic layer for bridge dialogue, yearly reflection, and complementarity with science-of-science ranking approaches.
secondaryParagraph: The chapter closes with a concrete discussion agenda on reorganization criteria, interpretability thresholds, evidence integration, and boundary-to-field transitions.
cautionNote: Interpretability should be preserved when scaling to larger corpora.
figureCaption: Three implications blocks plus concluding synthesis.
